name: Prosciutto and Goat Cheese Timbales with Mixed Greens
recipeIngredient: 10 ounces soft fresh goat cheese, room temperature
8 ounces haricots verts, trimmed, cut crosswise in half
1/4 cup champagne vinegar or white wine vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Line six 2/3-cup ramekins with plastic wrap, leaving overhang.
2. In each ramekin, place 2 slices of prosciutto, overlapping in the center and patching any holes with extra prosciutto.
3. Stir goat cheese, cream cheese, and chopped green onions in a bowl until well blended. Season generously with pepper.
4. Spoon the mixture into the prepared ramekins, dividing equally. Fold prosciutto over to enclose it.
5. Cover ramekins with plastic wrap and press to compact.
6. Chill overnight.
7. Cook beans in a medium pot of boiling salted water until just crisp-tender, about 4 minutes. Drain; rinse with cold water and drain well. Pat dry. (Can be prepared up to 1 day ahead. Cover and chill.)
8. Invert ramekins to release timbales. Peel off the plastic.
9. Heat 1 tablespoon olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add timbales, bottom side down. Cover; cook until cheese is just soft when pressed, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a plate and let stand for 1 hour to set up.
10. Meanwhile, combine vinegar and shallots in a small bowl. Slowly whisk in 1/2 cup olive oil. Season with salt and pepper.
11. Combine greens, beans, chopped green onions, and dressing in a large bowl; toss to coat. Divide salad among 6 plates. Top each with timbale. Drizzle some of the remaining dressing over each.
